questions.answer: The given differential equation can be written as (dy/dx) = (y^2/x^2) - (y/x). This is a homogeneous equation. Put y = vx. Then, (dy/dx) = v + x(dv/dx). So, v + x(dv/dx) = (v^2) - v. Thus, x(dv/dx) = (v^2) - 2v. Separating the variables, we get (dv/(v^2 - 2v)) = (dx/x). Integrating both sides, ∫(dv/(v(v-2))) = ∫(dx/x). Using partial fractions, we get ∫((1/2)(dv/v) - (1/2)(dv/(v-2))) = ∫(dx/x). On integrating, we get (1/2)ln|v| - (1/2)ln|v-2| = ln|x| + ln|c|. So, ln|(v/(v-2))| = ln|(x^2*c)|. Thus, (y/x) / ((y/x) - 2) = c*x^2. Hence, y = (2x^3*c)/(1 - x^2*c).

questions.q: The general solution of the differential equation (dy/dx) = (x + y) / (x - y) is
Solve the differential equation: cos^2(x) * (dy/dx) + y * sin(2x) = 0
questions.answer: The given differential equation can be written as (dy/dx) + y*tan(x) = 0. This is a linear equation. The integrating factor is e^(∫tan(x)dx) = e^(-ln|cos(x)|) = (1/cos(x)). Multiplying both sides by the integrating factor, we get d(y*(1/cos(x)))/dx = 0. Integrating both sides, y*(1/cos(x)) = c. Thus, y = c*cos(x).

The solution of the differential equation dy/dx = (y + x) / (y - x) is
Find the particular solution of the differential equation (dy/dx) = (3x - 2y) / (x + y) given that y = 1 when x = 0
questions.answer: The given differential equation is (dy/dx) = (3x - 2y) / (x + y). This is a homogeneous equation. Put y = vx. Then, (dy/dx) = v + x(dv/dx). So, v + x(dv/dx) = (3x - 2vx) / (x + vx). Thus, x(dv/dx) = ((3 - 2v) / (1 + v)) - v. Separating the variables, we get ((1 + v) / (3 - 3v))dv = (dx/x). Integrating both sides, ∫((1 + v) / (3 - 3v))dv = ∫(dx/x). Using partial fractions, we get ∫((1/3)(dv/v) + (2/3)(dv/(1 - v))) = ∫(dx/x). On integrating, we get (1/3)ln|v| - (2/3)ln|1 - v| = ln|x| + ln|c|. So, ln|(v/(1 - v)^2)| = ln|(x^3*c)|. Thus, (y/x) / ((1 - y/x)^2) = c*x^3. Given y = 1 when x = 0. Substituting these values in the equation above, we get (1/0) / ((1 - 1/0)^2) = c*0^3. So, c = 1. Hence, (y/x) / ((1 - y/x)^2) = x^3.
